properties.name: Rarawa Beach Campsite
properties.note: Directions: 3 km north of Ngataki along SH1 turn into Rarawa Beach Rd. Access: 4 km of gravel. Note: Take care in the water as the beach may be dangerous.

properties.description: This large campground is in the Tāwharanui Open Sanctuary. It's situated on the north eastern coast behind the sand dunes and between Phoenix Reef and Comet Rock. The campground is divided into five bays with a central road. It has a flat, undulating gras
properties.name: Tāwharanui Campground

properties.description: This is a boat-in only campsite on the Whanganui Journey. There is no drive on access.
properties.name: Mangapapa Campsite

properties.description: This is a boat-in only campsite on the Whanganui Journey. There is no drive on access.
properties.name: Poukaria Campsite

properties.description: This is a boat-in only campsite on the Whanganui Journey. There is no drive on access.
properties.name: John Coull Campsite

properties.description: This campsite is located in Tīeke Marae/Kāinga. This area offers a unique cultural experience
properties.name: Tīeke Marae/kāinga Campsite

properties.description: This is a beach front campsite in a farm setting.  An ideal location for water sports, including swimming, boating, diving and fishing.
properties.name: Fletcher Bay Campsite

properties.description: Camp in peaceful surroundings beneath pōhutukawa trees. A track with outstanding views leads to the bushline.
properties.name: Fantail Bay Campsite

properties.description: This is a spacious (5 ha) campsite beside the coast. Walk or mountain bike to Fletcher Bay along the Coromandel Coastal Walkway. Good fishing and diving nearby.
properties.name: Stony Bay Campsite

properties.description: This campsite is located beside the Waitawheta River, known for its trout and some great swimming holes downstream. There is a year-round liquor ban at the campsite.
properties.name: Dickey Flat Campsite

properties.description: Camp beside the beautiful lake in a grassy area. Walk, 4WD or mountain bike from here, or take a swim in the icy waters!
properties.name: Loch Katrine Campsite

properties.description: NZMCA Members only
properties.name: NZMCA Park

properties.description: Take a break at the northern foot of Raetea, a lowland coastal forest situated in the Mangamuka Gorge.
properties.name: Raetea North Side Campsite

properties.description: Camp beside a sandy beach among pōhutukawa trees. A great spot for water activities.
properties.name: Waikahoa Bay Campsite

properties.description: Enjoy the picturesque campsite above the Tauherenikau River set in regenerating bush. Camp at the road end or walk approx 150 m along the track to a grassy area amongst trees. Walk the Tauherenikau Gorge track or cool off with a swim in the river.
properties.name: Bucks Road Campsite

properties.description: At the eastern park entrance beneath the Tararua Ranges. Enjoy walks, fishing and swimming nearby.  There are also grassed areas for sports.
properties.name: Holdsworth Campsite

properties.description: Walk-in or boat-in to this beachside campsite on the Waiharakeke to Whariwharangi section of the Abel Tasman Coast Track. It is only available for booking for people walking the Coastal Track.
properties.name: Tōtaranui Great Walk Campsite

properties.description: A peaceful campsite beside a stream on the edge of the Tongariro National Park.
properties.name: Mangahuia Campsite

properties.description: A basic campsite in the Kaimanawa Forest Park. Many forest walks start nearby.
properties.name: Kaimanawa Road Campsite

properties.description: A standard campsite above the Pohangina River near the Ruahine Forest Park
properties.name: Pohangina Base Campsite

properties.description: This is a good place to stop for lunch, with a shelter, fireplace and water.
properties.name: Verns Camp

properties.description: This is a boat-in only campsite on the Whanganui Journey. There is no drive on access.
properties.name: Maharanui Campsite

properties.description: Camp near a stream beside lush forest and look out for native wildlife such as kākā, kōkako, kākāriki and short-tailed bats.
properties.name: Ngaherenga Campsite

properties.description: A serviced campground in the Kauri Coast area. Camp beside a small but beautiful kauri stand. There are many walking tracks nearby.
properties.name: Trounson Kauri Park Campground
Tapotupotu Campsite
properties.note: Closed due to slips on Tapotupotu Road

properties.description: Pae o te Rangi is a working farm with spectacular views. The campsite is grassed and is a north-facing open site.
properties.name: Pae o te Rangi Campground

properties.description: Sited beside the Putangirua Stream with views of Cook Strait and a pebbly beach across the road. Walk from here to the impressive Putangirua Pinnacles, or drive to the seal colony near Cape Palliser lighthouse.
properties.name: Putangirua Pinnacles Campsite

properties.description: Large, flat and sheltered vehicle-based campground. It is set back from a beautiful bay and child-friendly beach. There are many places to explore and you'll get tent-side viewing of the bird life.
properties.name: Poukaraka Flats Campground

properties.description: Freedom camping in designated area
properties.name: Onewhero Domain

properties.description: Offers boat ride across the bay for TA hikers.
properties.name: Nikau Bay Camp

properties.description: A back-to-basics grass surface campground set near the park's woolshed. The campground is located on rolling pastures with a backdrop of forest clad hills and a bush stream nearby.
properties.name: Barn Paddock Campground

properties.description: Back-to-basics large grass clearing in the Karamatura Valley. The campground sits next to a stream. It can only be access on foot.
properties.name: Karamatura Valley Campground

properties.description: This is a basic campsite in the Waitomo/Pureora area. From here, explore forest roads by car or mountain bike.
properties.name: Piropiro Campsite

properties.description: Camp at the eastern edge of the forest, close to kauri trees. Look out for kiwi and short-tailed bats. This is an ideal base for day or overnight tramps.
properties.name: Puketi Recreation Area Campsite

properties.description: This is a Great Walk campsite in the Whanganui area. This is one of four road access points to the Whanganui Journey. Look and listen for kererū, tūi and fantail. Brown kiwi can often be heard at night.
properties.name: Ohinepane Campsite

properties.description: Basic grassed campground situated between dune wetlands and coastal cliffs. The campground is located near an old logging tramway tunnel.
properties.name: Tunnel Campsite

properties.description: Grassed campground at the foot of a hill with an open shelter. The reserve is a scientific reserve owned by DoC and managed by Auckland Council. It is a spectacular area of coastal dunes and wetlands.
properties.name: Pararaha Valley Campsite

properties.description: Small tent sites, a short walk from the car park. Campsites are accessible from the car park by foot only. There is an open shelter with a bench.
properties.name: McCreadies Paddock Campsite

properties.description: for self-contained vehicles only
properties.name: Whakaipō Bay Recreation Reserve
Arataki Visitor Centre CSC overnight parking area

properties.description: Camp beside a high-country lake in the Waimakariri Basin; a good base for bird watching.
properties.name: Lake Pearson (Moana Rua) Campsite
Camp Tuhikaramea
properties.operator: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

properties.description: Free overnight camping for self contained vehicles
Freedom camping for self contained vehicles

properties.name: Te Araroa NZMCA Park East Cape

properties.description: Camp next to a beautiful beach and alongside a stream. The site is surrounded by forest and coastal bush. You must provide your own chemical toilet at this site.
properties.name: Anaura Bay Campsite

properties.description: This is a basic campsite in the Hawke's Bay region. Beside the Waikare River, this grassy campsite is a 10 minute walk to the beach for swimming, fishing and boating.
properties.name: Waikare River Mouth Campsite

properties.description: Open 24/7, but camping is prohibited between 18 December and 8 February
properties.name: Sandy Beach Camp
properties.note: Tag opening times from description, then remove the description.

properties.description: Ta Rauone Reserve - self contained vehicle camping
Self contained vehicles only
properties.name: Freedom Camping Three Sisters

properties.description: This sheltered and secluded campsite is the site of an old gold mining town. Explore the gold mining area and try your luck fossicking in the nearby creek.
properties.name: Goldsborough Campsite

properties.description: Freedom camping for certified self-contained vehicles in designated area
properties.name: Buxton Carpark self contained camping
Pipiriki Camping Grounds
properties.note: Was Pipiriki School

properties.description: This scenic camp is a great base for boating, fishing, swimming and kayaking and is the gateway to Rangitoto ki te Tonga/D’Urville Island.
properties.name: French Pass Campsite
West Oxford Reserve
properties.note: Must be self-contained, no fresh water. Only amenity is a toilet

properties.description: Free campsite. Beautiful beech forest, hung with mistletoe in summer, grows right up to the lake edge.
properties.name: Round Bush Campsite

properties.description: Freedom camping area managed by Westland District Council. Free for self-contained vehicles and non-self-contained campers. No facilities. High flood risk during rain. Maximum 3 consecutive nights and 5 nights total per month.
properties.name: Whataroa Gorge Freedom Camping
properties.source: Westland District Council freedom camping policy 2024

properties.description: Free overnight parking for campervans, caravans & cars; small site with limited spaces; register on arrival at bar/restaurant after 16:00; use of on-site toilets & shower; consumption appreciated but not mandatory
properties.name: Woodstock Hotel & Brewpub (register at bar after 16:00)

properties.description: Free overnight parking for certified self-contained campervans only; dump station & bins; potable water; no toilets/showers; 3-night max; ~11–15 sites
properties.name: Grey River Overnight Campground

properties.description: Rest spot beside the Takaka River; free camping
properties.name: Uruwhenua Reserve Free Camping

properties.description: Walk-in or boat-in to this campsite on the Waiharakeke to Whariwharangi section of the Abel Tasman Coast Track. There is no drive on access.
properties.name: Waiharakeke Bay Campsite

properties.description:
Non-self-contained vehicles permitted.
No more than 2 vehicles camping at any time.
Tents are prohibited.
A person may only freedom camp at a particular freedom camping site for a maximum of two nights within a 30-day period.
properties.name: Chisholm Park Carpark (2 non-self-contained vehicles)
Matahiwi Coffee Shop and Roadside Cabins

properties.description: This scenic, riverside campsite is an ideal base for exploring the Valley. A replica kauri dam is a 20 minute walk away.
properties.name: Shag Stream Campsite

properties.description: For self contained vehicles only. Camp across the road from the river and explore the Kauaeranga Valley on one of the many short walks.
properties.name: Kahikatea Campsite

properties.note: freedom of camping

properties.description: Self-contained only, 4 vehicles limit, maximum 24 hours
properties.name: Te Corner
